# Xiaohei IP

## Character Definition

Xiaohei is the fixed IP of this illustration set, but it is not a rigid logo, nor a mascot. It is an absurd little figure earnestly working on-site amid real objects.

It can have subtle differences in body shape across scenes: a bit taller, a bit shorter, a bit fatter, a bit thinner, a bit rounder, a bit more crooked. The variation should be small, while recognition must stay stable.

## Identifying Features That Must Be Preserved

- Solid black body.
- Small white round-dot eyes.
- Thin arms, thin legs.
- Slightly irregular outline, with a hand-drawn feel.
- Expression empty, blank, calm, earnest.
- No mouth, or only a minimal short-line mouth.
- No complex clothing.
- No exaggerated expressions.

## 2.0 Body Flexibility

Default form:

- Black bean / soft capsule / little black shadow.
- Height-to-width ratio roughly 1.5:1 to 2.2:1.
- Can be slightly fatter than 1.0, to give it more of a lived-in feel.
- Height usually stays at 8%-13% of the frame height; don't suddenly turn it into a giant protagonist.

Allowed variations:

- Can become a bit thinner when stretched.
- Can become a bit flatter when pressed down.
- Can become shorter and stockier when carrying things.
- Can show only half of its black silhouette when burrowing into an object.
- Can deform when being filtered/sucked in/clamped, but must still be recognizable as Xiaohei.

Forbidden variations:

- Don't turn it into a cute mascot.
- Don't turn it into a children's cartoon.
- Don't give it exaggerated big eyes, a smiley face, or meme-style facial features.
- Don't dress it in a suit, hat, or complex gear to explain its identity.
- Don't over-anthropomorphize it.
- Don't make every image the exact same pose and the exact same proportion, otherwise it becomes rigid.

## Xiaohei Must Carry the Core Action

Xiaohei cannot just stand to the side watching.

Qualified actions:

- Being pulled into the computer by a meeting card.
- Holding a piece of cardboard to block a message note.
- Untying the knot in an alarm cable.
- Editing a draft under a magnifying glass.
- Holding back a work badge stamped "Automated".
- Pushing a résumé sheet toward a sieve.
- Being yanked back by an off-work cable.
- Stuffing material into a machine or pulling it out of an object.

Disqualified actions:

- Standing in a corner looking at objects.
- Holding up a sign to explain the theme.
- Posing for a photo with objects.
- Having no physical contact with the real objects.
- The image's meaning still being complete after Xiaohei is removed.

Judgment criterion:

```text
If you delete Xiaohei and the metaphor still fully holds, it means Xiaohei has failed.
```

## Personality

- Earnest, doesn't put on cuteness.
- A little clumsy, but not stupid.
- Like a low-key system operator.
- Very calm amid absurd scenes.
- Its cuteness comes from "earnestly doing absurd things", not from expressions and decorations.
